claim: LangSmith deployments support custom authentication handlers (auth.authenticate, auth.on, auth.on_resource) to enforce multi-tenancy without custom middleware
actionable: Use LangSmith's auth decorators to inject user context and filter resources by ownership automatically on every request

claim: Multi-tenant agents can be built with minimal code (3 decorators, ~40 lines of Python) when using a framework that supports auth handlers
actionable: If building agents, use frameworks with built-in auth decorators to scope resources by user instead of rolling custom auth in agent code

claim: Permission-based access control (e.g., only admins can create crons) can be enforced at individual API endpoint level, not just resource level
actionable: Create role-based access control by adding auth handlers to specific endpoints/methods, not just resource operations

claim: Stateless validation of tokens from external auth providers (like Supabase) avoids database queries on every request
actionable: Use JWT tokens or auth provider APIs for stateless validation; avoid querying your database for user auth on every request
